instructions: Each label is based on all eight ordered frames in the corresponding preregistered development-window manifest row.

assignments.rationale: The wearer turns from the speaker to the refrigerator and opens it, compatible with retrieving and bringing creamer, but transport of the requested item is not shown.
Across the strip the wearer eats while watching a phone; no ECU tab is pressed, and the verb comes from the phone tutorial.
The wearer continuously stirs a bowl; no reading action occurs and 'read my mind' is figurative.
The wearer watches another person handle a coffee maker and does not visibly serve anything.
The wearer holds and faces a phone screen throughout, compatible with watching, but the spoken target is a narrated light in the phone video rather than an unambiguous wearer action label.
The wearer eats while holding a phone; no wiping occurs and 'wiped down the dinosaurs' is narration from the phone.
The wearer moves a place setting and nearby items as the table is being cleared, compatible with cleaning up, but the exact cleaning action and agency are ambiguous.
The wearer handles a container or board near food but never cuts; the lexical anchor is from 'cutting board'.
The wearer manipulates a lidded spread container and ends holding it open, visibly instantiating opening.
The wearer reaches into an open refrigerator and takes an item; no setting or placing action is shown.
The wearer observes another person at the counter and does not visibly wash an object.
The wearer faces a seated person during a drink offer; neither the wearer nor the visible person drinks in the strip.
The view remains on an entertainment unit and room; no wearer play action is visible, and the utterance describes someone else having played.
The wearer positions a pistachio bag over a bowl and dispenses its contents, clearly matching pour.
The wearer remains seated and turns the head away from the television while another person is instructed to walk by.
The wearer approaches the counter and takes two mugs into the hands, clearly matching grab.
The wearer eats while watching a phone; no fuse-box lid is removed and the utterance comes from the phone tutorial.
The wearer holds a phone showing a video and does not visibly turn notifications on; the instruction is phone-video audio.
Another person handles a pan at the stove while the wearer observes; the wearer does not cook.
The wearer gestures during conversation at a table but does not eat; the utterance is a prohibition about eating.
A seated other person holds the offered drink while the wearer remains opposite; the wearer does not take it.
The wearer visibly repositions a small craft object immediately after saying 'Let's move this.'
The wearer observes another person at a coffee maker; no wearer making action is visible.
The wearer carries a bowl or container across the counter area and places it at the stated location.
The wearer walks from the hall into the kitchen while saying 'Getting my coffee,' compatible with getting it, but acquisition of coffee is not shown.
Another person handles the coffee maker and cup while the wearer observes; the wearer does not serve.
The wearer continuously holds a phone screen in view while eating, clearly matching the stated act of watching YouTube.
The wearer eats and watches a phone; no wiping occurs and 'wiped out life' is narration from the phone.
Another person carries a bowl away while the wearer holds a phone; no wearer cleaning action is visible.
The wearer gestures during conversation and does not cut; 'get a cut' refers to an injury.
Another person handles the cooking supplies while the wearer points and observes; the wearer does not open the oil.
The wearer eats while watching a phone tutorial; no wire is set aside by the wearer.
The wearer carries a prepared bowl away from the counter; no washing occurs, and the utterance declines the suggested wash ('No').
A second person approaches holding a beverage during an offer; the wearer does not drink.
The wearer points a remote at a television/menu; no wearer playing action is shown, and the question asks whom someone would play for.
A bowl of pistachios, open bag, and reaching hands are visible, compatible with the just-described pour, but the actual pouring interval is not captured.
The wearer remains seated while the view shifts away from the television; 'walking through' refers to another person.
The wearer leaves the table toward the kitchen immediately after saying they will grab pretzels, but the object pickup is outside the strip.
The wearer cuts and eats food while watching a phone tutorial; no battery box is removed.
The temporal view rotates from the television toward the wall in direct response to 'turn your head,' clearly matching turn.
Another person works at the stove and opens a cabinet while the wearer observes; no wearer cooking action occurs.
The wearer converses beside food but does not eat in the strip; the utterance says the referenced food was not eaten for dinner.
The wearer holds a phone showing an automotive video; 'take you over the sound limits' is tutorial narration, not a wearer taking action.
The wearer faces an entertainment unit and operates a remote; no object or body movement matching move is visible, and 'moves' is used as a noun in the transcript.
The wearer holds a phone showing a video; 'make sure' is part of the video's subscription request and is not a visible making action.
Another person handles and carries a mug while the wearer observes; no wearer putting action is visible.
The wearer remains seated and gestures during conversation; no getting or retrieval action occurs in the strip.
The wearer moves the plate and table items while the other person stands to clear the table, compatible with cleaning up, but the exact cleaning action is not fully visible.
